---
title: "Using Repository Skills"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using Repository Skills}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Purpose

This vignette is for maintainers who use coding assistants in the `vldaft`
repository. Repository skills keep task-specific working rules close to the
codebase, so a prompt can name a skill instead of restating the package model,
build system, validation expectations, and documentation conventions every
time.

The short rule is:

1. Read `inst/site/llms.txt` for the durable package guide.
2. Choose the repo-local skill that matches the task.
3. Inspect the relevant package files before editing.
4. Validate the narrow surface that changed.

These skills are contributor workflow aids. They do not affect the installed R
package or the runtime behavior of `vldaft()`.

## Skill map

| Skill | Use it for | Typical validation |
|---|---|---|
| `$vldaft-development` | R interface changes, C/Rust backend work, model implementation, backend parity, tests, and package checks. | `devtools::test()`, targeted `testthat`, `R CMD INSTALL .`, and C/Rust parity checks when compiled code changes. |
| `$vldaft-docs-site` | Roxygen, Rd files, README updates, vignettes, pkgdown navigation, and rendered-site verification. | Render the edited vignette or article, run `devtools::document()` for roxygen changes, and check pkgdown output when navigation or site assets change. |

When a task spans both skills, start with the one that owns the first edit.
For example, a new exported argument usually starts as `$vldaft-development`
and then switches to `$vldaft-docs-site` once the behavior and tests are
settled.

## Example prompts

Use a skill name at the front of the request when you want the assistant to
load that workflow before acting.

```text
Use $vldaft-development to add a theta_vars regression test that checks the
C and Rust back ends agree on a simulated right-censored Weibull dataset.
```

```text
Use $vldaft-docs-site to add a vignette section explaining the log-likelihood
Jacobian adjustment and render the updated article locally.
```

```text
Use $vldaft-development, then $vldaft-docs-site, to add a new prediction type
for cure probability quantiles, test it, document it, and update the README
example.
```

## Worked documentation task

A documentation task should be intentionally narrow. For a request like
"add a vignette demonstrating skill usage", the assistant should:

1. Read `inst/site/llms.txt`.
2. Inspect the existing files in `vignettes/` and `_pkgdown.yml`.
3. Add the vignette with standard R vignette metadata.
4. Add the article slug to `_pkgdown.yml` if it should appear on the site.
5. Render the new vignette, preferably to a temporary output directory.
6. Review the diff and leave unrelated worktree changes untouched.

The validation command can stay focused on the new article:

```r
rmarkdown::render(
  "vignettes/agent-skills.Rmd",
  output_dir = tempdir(),
  quiet = TRUE
)
```

For roxygen or reference-site changes, broaden validation to include
documentation generation or a pkgdown article build.

## Worked implementation task

Implementation changes have a wider blast radius because `vldaft` has both C
and Rust computational back ends. A backend task should use the development
skill and explicitly check whether an algorithmic change must be mirrored.

For example, a request to change theta-polynomial handling should guide the
assistant to:

1. Read the R boundary in `R/vldaft.R`.
2. Inspect the C partials engine in `src/loscreg8.c`.
3. Inspect the Rust counterpart in `src/rust/src/model.rs`.
4. Update registration or wrappers if the call contract changes.
5. Add or adjust tests under `tests/testthat/`.
6. Run targeted parity tests before broader package checks.

That mirrors the core contract in `inst/site/llms.txt`: R interface semantics,
C entry points, Rust wrappers, and registration must move together when the
backend contract changes.

## Prompt template

For routine work, this template is usually enough:

```text
Use $SKILL_NAME to <make the specific change>. Read inst/site/llms.txt first,
preserve unrelated worktree changes, and run the narrow validation that matches
the edited files.
```

Replace `$SKILL_NAME` with the relevant repo-local skill. Add any constraints
that matter for the task, such as "do not rebuild pkgdown" or "compare C and
Rust output on the existing simulated dataset."
